title: tk next: surface unblocked childless epics as a planning action
description:
Spec: docs/design/execution-model-v2-epic-chaining-and-learning-loop.md §1 'tk changes', resolving Open Question 4 additively. Consumes query.EpicsNeedingPlanning (already implemented in internal/query/plan.go when this tick runs).

File: cmd/tk/cmd/next.go.

Behavior (agent mode only — NOT when --awaiting is set):
- After computing 'ready' and finding it EMPTY, before printing 'No ready ticks': compute query.EpicsNeedingPlanning over the same filter scope (global, --epic, or the parent epic when EPIC_ID arg given — for the EPIC_ID form, check whether that epic itself needs planning). If non-empty, sort with query.SortByPriorityCreatedAt and return the first.
- Ready tasks always win over planning candidates; planning is the fallback when nothing is implementable.

Output (additive JSON change):
- Introduce a wrapper struct: type nextOutput struct { tick.Tick; Action string `json:"action"` } and use it for ALL --json output from tk next: action="implement" for a ready task, action="plan" for an epic needing planning. Existing consumers that decode into a tick shape are unaffected (extra key).
- Human output for planning result: <id>  P<n> epic  <title>  (needs planning — no child ticks). Human output for ready tasks unchanged.
- Update the command's Long help text with one line about the planning fallback.

Tests: extract any non-trivial selection logic so it is unit-testable; if cmd/tk/cmd has no test convention, verify via a Go test against the wrapper marshaling (action key present alongside tick fields) plus an end-to-end check: build tk (go build ./cmd/tk), create a throwaway git repo + .tick fixture in /tmp with one childless unblocked epic, run 'tk next --json' and 'tk next <epic-id> --json', assert action==plan. Document the manual check commands in your report.

Run: go test ./... && go build ./...
acceptance_criteria: go test ./... passes; tk next --json returns action:plan for a childless unblocked epic and action:implement for ready tasks (fixture-verified)
